summary:
The least useful diagnostic tool among options provided for identifying specific aspects related to spondylolisthesis such as bone detail without adequate soft tissue assessment is an X-ray lateral view; MRI provides more comprehensive imaging including both bones and associated structures crucially involved with this condition. \boxed{\text{X-ray}} Agent2 concludes by emphasizing why a plain X-ray Lateral View (Option C) stands out as less informative than other modalities when evaluating all pertinent anatomical areas impacted by conditions like spondylosis listheresis where precise evaluation includes not just osseous but also neurovascular considerations best captured via advanced techniques offering enhanced soft tissue contrast. 

Final Summary:

- Plain X-ray (Lateral View), though showing basic vertebral positioning well enough initially, falls short given need for thorough examination involving intricate spinal cord/nerve root relationships which require higher sensitivity from Magnetic Resonance Imaging(MRI). Hence,
The most inadequate choice amongst listed diagnostics tools aimed at pinpointing exact nature & extent of spondylolysis lies within simple radiographic methods lacking requisite multiplanar clarity offered through modern non-invasive alternatives capable capturing full spectrum pathology implicated herein. 
Thus summarized succinctly yet comprehensively addressing core issue presented originally concerning utility ranking across various medical imaging approaches utilized clinically today towards establishing definitive diagnoses pertaining musculoskeletal disorders affecting lower back region notably characterized pathologically speaking by slippage phenomenon known medically under term 'spondylolithesis'. Final Answer:\(\boxed{\text{{Plain X-Ray}}}.\)

Key Points Summarized In Two Sentences:
A straightforward x-ray cannot provide sufficient information about underlying neurological impacts essential during differential diagnosis process especially considering complexity inherent in managing suspected instances of spondylolisthetic degeneration necessitating integration multiple complementary views alongside direct observation into intervertebral disc health status etc., thereby rendering said modality inferior relative others available currently used extensively throughout orthopedic practice settings globally aiming accurate staging classification thereof .
